improvements_this_run: Day scene lighting fixed: v9:4.75 → v11:75 (+1500% improvement, +70.25 points)
Night scene improved: v10:1.5 → v11:63 (+4100% improvement, +61.5 points)
GLB model imports successful: All 4 vehicle types (sedan, SUV, van, police) visible and properly lit
3-point area lighting technique deployed: 500W/400W/500W configuration for day scenes
Session checkpoint system created for run cohesion and historical tracking
Skill exercise system created (5 exercises for Blender fundamentals)
YouTube skill translator operational (14 tutorials identified, translation complete)
Sodium vapor spot lights operational for night scenes (+48 points from v10)

remaining_problems.description: Scorer reports 'no meaningful edge detail detected' on all renders (edge_density 0.003-0.008)
remaining_problems.root_cause: Likely models missing subdivision surface modifier or viewer disabled, or models scaled too large
remaining_problems.fix_priority: CRITICAL - blocks advancement to materials phase
remaining_problems.estimated_score_impact: Could gain 10-15 points with proper geometry detail

remaining_problems.description: Scene 4 night renders at 63 (4 scenes 73-78, but night stuck at 63). Contrast histogram_spread: 0.3765 vs day 0.8431
remaining_problems.root_cause: Sodium vapor SPOT lights insufficient contrast; need better fill light or HDRI adjustment
remaining_problems.estimated_score_impact: Could reach 70+ with HDRI environment lighting

remaining_problems.description: DriverPOV angles show reduced brightness (avg_brightness 0.15-0.32 vs birdeye 0.36-0.59)
remaining_problems.root_cause: Camera position inside vehicle cab receiving less direct light
remaining_problems.estimated_score_impact: Could gain 5-8 points with dedicated cabin lighting

remaining_problems.description: All vehicle models using default gray material (no texture, no metallic, no roughness)
remaining_problems.root_cause: GLB import but no material assignment in Blender
remaining_problems.estimated_score_impact: Could gain 15-25 points with proper materials

remaining_problems.description: No evidence markers, scale bars, or impact deformation visible on any render
remaining_problems.root_cause: Infrastructure not created yet (annotation system, impact sim not configured)
remaining_problems.estimated_score_impact: forensic_clarity currently 6.5, could reach 8.0+ with markers

remaining_problems.description: World background is solid 0.3-0.4 gray (no HDRI, no sky simulation)
remaining_problems.root_cause: Default Blender world setting, no Polyhaven HDRI applied
remaining_problems.fix_priority: MEDIUM - improves realism but not critical for scoring
remaining_problems.estimated_score_impact: Could gain 5 points with proper HDRI

forensic_clarity.assessment: Models visible and properly lit, but no markers, labels, or evidence annotations. Composition supports investigation but lacks overlay details.

physical_plausibility.assessment: Models are real GLB geometry with proper lighting, but no deformation, no material properties, and no environmental interaction. Looks staged.

cinematic_presentation.assessment: Composition is strong (day scenes 75+ avg). Lighting is cinematic for day scenes. But night scenes flat, and no environmental context (HDRI, sky).

v9.what_changed: Full rewrite with GLB models, v2/v3 lighting refinements

v10.what_failed: Primitive geometry (cubes for vehicles), no proper import

v11_cycles.cycle2: v2 lighting fix - 50 avg (sodium vapor added for night)
v11_cycles.cycle3: v3 lighting fix - 71.1 avg (3-point fine-tuning, day/night balance)

harsh_findings.summary: v11 has solved the critical rendering pipeline problems from v10. Models are visible, lighting is functional, and day scenes are reaching acceptable quality (75 avg). However, the pipeline is still in early foundation phase. Edge detail detection failures, missing materials, and zero forensic annotations mean the renders are 'pretty pictures' not forensic documentation.
harsh_findings.core_issue: Scorer reports 'no meaningful edge detail detected' on ALL 12 renders. This single issue is preventing advancement beyond 73-78. Either models lack subdivision, or viewer needs adjustment, or geometry is under-detailed. This MUST be resolved before materials phase.
harsh_findings.quality_ceiling: Current scoring ceiling is ~78 without solving edge detail problem. Materials phase could add 15-25 points if edge detail is fixed. Forensic detail phase could add another 10-15 points.

readiness_assessment.ready_for_materials_phase: NO (edge detail issue must be fixed first)

critical_blockers: BLOCKER 1: Edge detail detection failure - must diagnose why all renders report edge_density 0.003-0.008
BLOCKER 2: PBR materials not applied - all vehicles still gray default
BLOCKER 3: No forensic infrastructure - evidence markers not implemented

next_run_priorities: CRITICAL: Investigate edge detail issue - check if models need subdivision modifier or if viewer comp needs adjustment
CRITICAL: Apply PBR materials to all vehicle models using Blender Shader Editor
CRITICAL: Build evidence marker system (position, orientation, visibility via Python script)
HIGH: Download Polyhaven HDRI and apply to world background (improves night scene contrast from 0.37 to 0.50+)
HIGH: Create impact deformation system for collision scenes (Scene 1 and 3)
MEDIUM: Add cabin lighting to improve DriverPOV angles (currently 66-74, target 75+)
MEDIUM: Add scale bar generation system (rendered geometry + overlay)
BACKLOG: Particle systems for dust/debris (polish phase)
audit_confidence: HIGH - all 12 renders successfully scored with consistent methodology. Scores are comparable across versions.
